body:
## Summary
- Link `agent/memory` and `agent/rubrics` refs in the prompt fragments agents receive.
- Link the configured rubrics branch in rubrics-update summary comments.
- Add focused coverage for prompt branch URLs and rubrics comment formatting.

## Verification
- `npm --prefix .agent run build && node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/response.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js`

Closes #42

comments.body:
Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `implement` -> `review` (round 3/10).

agent planner selected review: Implementation succeeded and produced PR #53 for issue #42; no review has run yet and there is no newer signal to stop.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777523209551 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUxNDcxODgyMzc6aW1wbGVtZW50OjQyOnJldmlldzo1Mzoz -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 5 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The PR extends existing `formatRubricsUpdateComment` behavior in `.agent/src/response.ts` and wires the existing `prepare-rubrics-update-summary` CLI instead of adding a new comment path. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR body closes #42, whose title is exactly the requested nit: “when referring to agent/rubrics or agent/memory branch, be sure to add the link for it.” The diff directly addresses that linked context. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | The diff is limited to prompt fragments, the rubrics-update summary formatter/CLI wiring, and focused tests. No unrelated refactor or broad reformatting appears in the PR diff. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Prompt docs in `.github/prompts/_memory.md` and `.github/prompts/_rubrics.md` now link `${MEMORY_REF}` and `${RUBRICS_REF}` where the agent-facing docs mention those branches. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The implementation adds a small branch-reference formatter with repo-slug validation and a fallback to code formatting when no repo slug is available. Coverage verifies linked and fallback output. |

## Notes

- The strongest score drivers are the narrow scope and the focused coverage: `envelope.test.ts` checks the prompt branch URLs, and `response.test.ts` checks linked rubrics-update comments plus fallback behavior.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric violations found. The PR satisfies the applicable selected rubrics for this request.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis > Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**. > Requested by @lolipopshock. ## Summary of PR/Issue - PR #53 addresses issue #42, which asks that references to the `agent/memory` and `agent/rubrics` branches include links. - The PR links memory and rubrics branch references in the shared prompt fragments agents receive. - It also updates rubrics-update summary comments to link the configured rubrics branch when repository context is available. - Focused tests were added for prompt branch URLs and rubrics comment formatting. ## Review The implementation is narrow and mostly satisfies the request, and the rubrics review found no policy or style violations. One agent-facing prompt still contains an unlinked `${MEMORY_REF}` reference, so the request is not fully covered yet. | Issue | Severity | Description | | -- | -- | -- | | Missed memory branch reference in `memory-scan.md` | WARNING | `.github/prompts/memory-scan.md` still says the workflow pushes edits to `${MEMORY_REF}` without linking it. This is another agent-facing branch reference and should be made consistent with the updated shared memory prompt. | ## Progress - The shared memory prompt now links `${MEMORY_REF}` in both the branch introduction and push guardrail. - The shared rubrics prompt now links `${RUBRICS_REF}`. - Rubrics-update summary comments now link the configured rubrics branch when `GITHUB_REPOSITORY` is available, with fallback behavior covered. - Existing PR discussion already includes a rubrics review with a PASS verdict. - No inline PR comment was posted because the remaining issue is in a file not changed by this PR, so there is no eligible changed line for inline feedback. ## Issue Details
Missed memory branch reference in memory-scan.md **Cause:** The PR updated `.github/prompts/_memory.md`, but `.github/prompts/memory-scan.md` is also an agent-facing prompt and still contains an unlinked `${MEMORY_REF}` reference at [`memory-scan.md#L30`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/d8c8cf1c6bb9b53cdc8ae6cc719d708256c728c5/.github/prompts/memory-scan.md#L30). **Candidate solutions:** Link `${MEMORY_REF}` there using the same pattern as `_memory.md`: `[${MEMORY_REF}](https://github.com/${REPO_SLUG}/tree/${MEMORY_REF})` **Comments:** This is a small coverage gap rather than a design disagreement. The independent rubrics review passed; the Codex review identified this missed prompt reference.
## Recommended Next Step `FIX_PR`: the remaining issue is a concrete, low-risk branch change suitable for an automated fix-pr pass. ## Final Verdict MINOR_ISSUES ## Action Items - [ ] Link the `${MEMORY_REF}` reference in `.github/prompts/memory-scan.md` so it matches the updated memory prompt behavior.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 4/10).

agent planner selected fix-pr: Review synthesis recommends FIX_PR for a concrete low-risk gap: an unlinked ${MEMORY_REF} reference remains in .github/prompts/memory-scan.md.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777523835650 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUxNDczNDkzOTU6cmV2aWV3OjUzOmZpeF9wcjo1Mzo0 -->
Note: I don't think we should update the prompts with the URLs

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `agent/implement-issue-42/codex-25147188237`. Requested by @@sepo-agent.

- Aligned the focused prompt-fragment test with the current PR direction after the maintainer removed prompt URL edits.
- Left `.github/prompts/memory-scan.md` unchanged because the latest branch state and maintainer note say not to add URLs in prompts.
- Verification passed: `npm --prefix .agent run build` and `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/response.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js`.

@sepo-agent /review it 

> Cause:
> The PR updated .github/prompts/_memory.md, but .github/prompts/memory-scan.md is also an agent-facing prompt and still contains an unlinked ${MEMORY_REF} reference at memory-scan.md#L30.
> 
> Candidate solutions:
> Link ${MEMORY_REF} there using the same pattern as _memory.md:
> [${MEMORY_REF}](https://github.com/${REPO_SLUG}/tree/${MEMORY_REF})
> 
> Comments:
> This is a small coverage gap rather than a design disagreement. The independent rubrics review passed; the Codex review identified this missed prompt reference.

I explicitly want to remove link reference in the prompts, since it doesn't really matter

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 5 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR context includes issue #42 plus the latest maintainer clarification: prompt URLs should be removed. Current PR diff no longer changes `.github/prompts/*`, matching that direction. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Current diff touches only `.agent/src/response.ts`, `.agent/src/cli/prepare-rubrics-update-summary.ts`, `.agent/src/__tests__/response.test.ts`, and `.github/workflows/agent-rubrics-update.yml`. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The change extends existing `formatRubricsUpdateComment` and the existing `prepare-rubrics-update-summary` CLI path instead of adding a parallel comment formatter. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The implementation adds a small branch-reference formatter with repo-slug validation and fallback formatting when no repo slug is available. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | No runtime behavior requiring prompt/doc updates remains in the current diff; the prompt-link direction was explicitly rejected by the requester. |

## Notes

- The current PR state aligns with the requester’s latest instruction: it does not add prompt links for `${MEMORY_REF}` or `${RUBRICS_REF}`.
- The remaining behavior is scoped to rubrics-update summary comments, with focused tests for linked output and fallback output.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric violations found. The earlier `memory-scan.md` prompt-link concern is not a rubric failure under the latest maintainer instruction to remove prompt link references.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #53 addresses issue #42, which asked for branch references to `agent/rubrics` or `agent/memory` to include links.
- After maintainer clarification, prompt fragments should not add URL links.
- The current PR now scopes the functional change to rubrics-update summary comments, linking the configured rubrics branch when repository context is available.
- It also adds focused formatter coverage for linked output and fallback behavior.

## Review
Both reviewer outputs agree there are no blocking or warning findings in the current PR state. The earlier `memory-scan.md` concern is superseded by the maintainer’s explicit direction not to add prompt URLs.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No actionable issues | INFO | Reviewers found no unresolved blocking or warning issues. The current diff aligns with the latest maintainer direction and remains narrowly scoped. |

## Progress
- Maintainer clarified prompt references should not be updated with URLs.
- Current PR diff no longer changes `.github/prompts/*`.
- Rubrics-update summary comments now link `RUBRICS_REF` when `GITHUB_REPOSITORY` is available.
- Fallback behavior without a repo slug is covered by tests.
- Existing rubrics review passed with no rubric-specific follow-up.

## Recommended Next Step
`NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION`: no unresolved actionable work remains for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
SHIP

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch changes.

## Rubrics Update

No changes were committed to [`agent/rubrics`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/tree/agent/rubrics) from PR #53.

no rubric changes

I reviewed PR #53, linked issue #42, issue comments, PR review comments, reviews, collaborator signals, and the existing rubric set. The only human preference in the PR was a scope clarification from `lolipopshock` about not adding branch URLs in prompt files. With `author_association: CONTRIBUTOR` and no admin/maintain collaborator signal returned, it is not enough to create a durable team rubric under the trust rules. Existing rubrics already cover the reusable lessons around linked context, surgical scope, and docs sync.

title: Link agent memory and rubrics branch references
